rulings.Case Title: DAVID R. ONGARO VS. VICTORIA GOLUNOVA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners David R. Ongaro, Ongaro Pcs Petition To Confirm Nonbinding Attorney-Client Fee Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION calendar for Tuesday, Dec-23-2025, LINE 11. PETITIONER DAVID ONGARO OF ONGARO PC Notice Of Hearing On Petitioners David R. Ongaro, Ongaro Pcs Petition To Confirm Nonbinding Attorney-Client Fee Arbitration Award Hearing Required. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. SHIMMICK CONSTRUCTION COMPANY, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: FURTHER HEAERING RE: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION calendar for Tuesday, Dec-23-2025, LINE 2. FURTHER HEARING RE: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Cross-defendant Precision Engineering, Inc. 's motion for summary judgment is granted. Precision satisfied its summary judgment burden by showing that the discovery responses of cross-complainant Shimmick Construction Company, Inc. are devoid of facts showing that any conduct of Precision is responsible for the construction defects complained of by plaintiff City and County of San Francisco and Shimmick. Because Shimmick has not submitted any evidence on this motion showing that Precision is responsible for those construction defects, Precision is entitled to summary judgment in its favor. This ruling does not apply to any claim that Shimmick may have that Precision breached a contractual duty to defend Shimmick for any aspect of this case up to October 10, 2025. That claim was not alleged in Shimmick's cross-complaint against Precision or addressed in the motion papers filed by Precision. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: IVAN RESENIKOFF VS. FCA US LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys' Fees, Costs And Expenses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION calendar for Tuesday, Dec-23-2025, LINE 6. PLAINTIFF IVAN RESENIKOFF Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorneys' Fees, Costs And Expenses Plaintiff Ivan Resnikoff's motion for attorneys' fees, costs and expenses is granted in large part. No opposition filed and, except for the request for a multiplier, good cause has been shown for all fees, costs and expenses requested. Plaintiff is awarded fees of $17,139.50 and costs and expenses of $5,141.85 for a total of $22,281.35. The court exercises its discretion to deny a multiplier because the plaintiff has not shown that the circumstances in this case warrant increasing the lodestar amount. Defendant FCA withdrew its motion to tax costs. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: NATHANEL LOWE VS. CHRISTIN CREIGHTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Christin Creighton And For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of; Declaration Of Blake Bostwick In Support Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Christin Creighton
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION calendar for Tuesday, Dec-23-2025, LINE 7. PLAINTIFF NATHANEL LOWE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Christin Creighton And For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of; Declaration Of Blake Bostwick In Support Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Defendant Christin Creighton Plaintiff' Nathaniel Lowe's motion to compel the deposition of defendant Christin Creighton and for monetary sanctions is granted. Creighton must appear for her deposition on a mutually convenient date no later than January 30, 2026. Creighton and her counsel must pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $2,460 to Lowe no later than January 30, 2026. Creighton's failure to offer any dates for her deposition lacks substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) rulings.Case Title: CLARENCE STOKES VS. NGM BIOPHARMACEUTICALS INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION calendar for Tuesday, Dec-23-2025, LINE 8 (1 of 2.) DEFENDANT NGM BIOPHARMACEUTICALS INC., JEAN-FREDERIC VIRET DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Defendants BGM Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. and Jean-Frederic Viret's demurrer to the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Clarence Stokes is overruled in its entirety. Notwithstanding the parties' failure to engage in sufficient meet and confer, the court exercises its discretion to decide this motion. Stokes has adequately alleged facts supporting claims for harassment in violation of FEHA and wrongful termination in violation of public policy and none of his claims are barred by the workers's compensation exclusivity rule. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION calendar for Tuesday, Dec-23-2025, LINE 8 (2 of 2.) DEFENDANT NGM BIOPHARMACEUTICALS INC., JEAN-FREDERIC VIRET MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT Defendants BGM Biopharmaceuticals, Inc. and Jean-Frederic Viret's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Clarence Stokes is denied. rulings.Case Title: BRADFORD SOLINSKY VS. THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the DISCOVERY / LAW AND MOTION calendar for Tuesday, Dec-23-2025, LINE 9. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff Bradford Solinsky is overruled. Solinsky has adequately alleged that the City is equitably estopped from asserting that Solinsky's claim is untimely filed. The fact that the City informed Solinsky of the applicable time requirements does not preclude the application of equitable estoppel. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
